templates.expositionText: I've had this recipe stuck in my head for three days now and it won't leave me alone. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} to make it happen.
templates.acceptText: Oh, wonderful. I've been thinking about this all day and now it might actually happen.
templates.declineText: That's fine. The craving will pass. Probably.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Perfect. Tonight I'm cooking.
templates.resolutionText: You're a lifesaver. Take {reward_item}. The kitchen smells incredible already, and that's on you.

templates.expositionText: I've been dying to eat some {quest_item} but I haven't had a chance to go out and get any. Could you bring me {gather_count}?
templates.acceptText: Really? That's made my whole afternoon better already.
templates.declineText: No trouble. I've gone without before.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Oh, wonderful. I've been thinking about this all day.
templates.resolutionText: Thank you, truly. Take {reward_item}. I'm going to sit down with this and not share a bite.

templates.expositionText: I promised {settlement_npc} I'd bring something nice for dinner tonight, but my cupboard's bare. Would you get me {gather_count} {quest_item}?
templates.acceptText: Oh, thank goodness. {settlement_npc} would never let me hear the end of it if I showed up empty-handed.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll figure something out. I always do.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Now I won't show up empty-handed. {settlement_npc} would never let me hear the end of it.
templates.resolutionText: You've saved my reputation. Here's {reward_item}. {settlement_npc} will never know how close I cut it, and I'd like to keep it that way.

templates.expositionText: Workshop's running low. I need {gather_count} {quest_item} before I can get back to work.
templates.acceptText: Good. I've been staring at empty bins all morning.
templates.declineText: Fair enough. I'll work around it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Good. That should keep me going for a while.
templates.resolutionText: Appreciate it. Take {reward_item} for your trouble.

templates.expositionText: I'm almost out of {quest_item} and I've got orders to fill. I could use {gather_count} if you're willing.
templates.acceptText: That takes a weight off. I was starting to fall behind on orders.
templates.declineText: Understood. I'll make do with what I have.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That'll do nicely. Back to it, then.
templates.resolutionText: Thanks. Here's {reward_item} for your time.

templates.expositionText: I went to grab {quest_item} this morning and the bin was empty. Could you round up {gather_count} for me?
templates.acceptText: Perfect. One less thing to worry about today.
templates.declineText: No harm in asking. I'll sort it out myself.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Perfect. I'll get these sorted right away.
templates.resolutionText: You've saved me a trip. Take {reward_item} for your trouble.

templates.expositionText: Could you grab {gather_count} {quest_item} for me? My back's not what it was and the walk would do me in.
templates.acceptText: Bless you. My back thanks you even more than I do.
templates.declineText: That's all right. I'll manage the walk somehow.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Ah, just what I needed. You're quicker than I'd have been.
templates.resolutionText: Much obliged. Take {reward_item} with my thanks.

templates.expositionText: I promised I'd have {quest_item} ready by this afternoon, but I can't leave the shop. Any chance you could bring me {gather_count}?
templates.acceptText: That's a relief. I was running out of time.
templates.declineText: Worth a try. I'll figure something out before the afternoon's up.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You're a natural. I'll have these put to use right away.
templates.resolutionText: Here's {reward_item}. You've earned it.

templates.expositionText: I've been meaning to gather {gather_count} {quest_item} all week, but things keep coming up. Would you mind?
templates.acceptText: Finally, I can stop putting it off. Appreciate it.
templates.declineText: Can't blame you. It's been on my list too long as it is.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Finally. I've been putting this off for too long.
templates.resolutionText: You're a good sort. Take {reward_item} for your trouble.

templates.expositionText: I've always wanted to see what {quest_item} looks like up close. Bring me {gather_count} and let me have a proper look?
templates.acceptText: Really? I didn't think anyone would go along with that. This should be interesting.
templates.declineText: Fair enough. It was a strange ask.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Oh, fascinating. I see why people make a fuss over these.
templates.resolutionText: Thanks for indulging me. Take {reward_item}. I've turned these over twice now and I still don't know what I was expecting.

templates.expositionText: I want to try something. Bring me {gather_count} {quest_item} and I'll show you if my idea works.
templates.acceptText: Excellent. I've been itching to try this and now I actually can.
templates.declineText: The idea will keep. I'll get to it eventually.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Excellent. Let's see if this idea holds up.
templates.resolutionText: That was worth it. Take {reward_item} for your trouble.

templates.expositionText: {settlement_npc} told me {quest_item} has all sorts of uses I never knew about. Could you get me {gather_count}? I want to find out for myself.
templates.acceptText: Good. {settlement_npc} made it sound fascinating and I've been curious ever since.
templates.declineText: That's fine. I'll ask {settlement_npc} to bring some next time.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Let's see what all the talk is about.
templates.resolutionText: I learned something today. Take {reward_item} with my thanks.

templates.expositionText: I left my {quest_item} somewhere last week and I've been kicking myself ever since. I think {target_npc} over in {target_npc_settlement} might have found it.
templates.acceptText: Oh, that would be such a relief. I've been kicking myself over it for days.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll get over there myself when I find the time.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You actually found it. I can't believe I let that wander off.
templates.resolutionText: Thank you, really. Take {reward_item} with my gratitude.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Oh, you're here about that {quest_item}? Yeah, I've got it right here. Figured someone would come looking eventually.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them to keep better track of their things. Safe travels.

templates.expositionText: I can't find my {quest_item} anywhere. Last I remember, I had it when I visited {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. Could you ask if they've seen it?
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been worrying about it more than I should.
templates.declineText: That's all right. It's my own fault for losing track of it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a relief. I was starting to think it was gone for good.
templates.resolutionText: You've got my gratitude. Here's {reward_item}.
templates.targetNpcOpener: The {quest_item}? It's been sitting on my shelf. I was wondering whose it was. Here you go.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Glad to get it off my hands, honestly. Take care.

templates.expositionText: Embarrassing, but I left my {quest_item} at {target_npc}'s place in {target_npc_settlement}. I keep meaning to go back for it but I never get around to it.
templates.acceptText: You'd really do that? I keep meaning to go myself but the days just slip away.
templates.declineText: I know, I know. I should just go get it. One of these days.
templates.expositionTurnInText: There it is. I missed this more than I expected.
templates.resolutionText: You did me a real kindness. Take {reward_item}. I'd have put this trip off another month, easy.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Right, the {quest_item}. I kept it safe. Figured they'd want it back eventually. Here.
templates.targetNpcCloser: No trouble at all. See you around.

templates.expositionText: I've been waiting on a {quest_item} from {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}. It should be ready by now. Would you mind picking it up?
templates.acceptText: Good. I've been waiting on that and it'll be nice to have it in hand.
templates.declineText: No trouble. It's not going anywhere.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's exactly what I was expecting. Looks good.
templates.resolutionText: Appreciate the legwork. Take {reward_item} for the miles.
templates.targetNpcOpener: You're picking up for someone in {settlement_name}? Right, the {quest_item}. It's all ready. Here you are.

templates.expositionText: {target_npc} was supposed to send over a {quest_item}, but it never showed up. Could you go to {target_npc_settlement} and grab it for me?
templates.acceptText: Oh, good. I was starting to wonder if they'd forgotten about me entirely.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll send word again and hope for the best.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Finally. I'd started to wonder if they forgot about me.
templates.resolutionText: Thanks for handling that. Take {reward_item} for your trouble.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Oh, was I supposed to send that over? My mistake. Here's the {quest_item}. All yours.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Sorry about the wait. Safe trip back.

templates.expositionText: I asked {target_npc} to set aside a {quest_item} for me at their place in {target_npc_settlement}. I just haven't had the time to go get it. Could you?
templates.acceptText: That saves me half a day's walk. I appreciate it.
templates.declineText: No trouble. It'll still be there when I finally make the trip.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Just what I needed. Thank you for making the trip.
templates.resolutionText: You saved me half a day's walk. Here's {reward_item} for your trouble. Next time {target_npc} asks, I'll go myself, promise.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Ah, you're here for the {quest_item}. I've had it set aside. Here, take it.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Happy to help. Have a good one.

templates.expositionText: I lent my {quest_item} to {target_npc} ages ago and I'd really like it back. They're over in {target_npc_settlement}. Would you ask them for it?
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I didn't want to make it awkward by asking them directly.
templates.declineText: Understandable. It's my problem, really.
templates.expositionTurnInText: There it is. Feels good to have it back.
templates.resolutionText: I owe you one. Take {reward_item} for your trouble.
templates.targetNpcOpener: The {quest_item}? Oh, right. I've been meaning to return that. Sorry it took so long. Here you go.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Tell them no hard feelings, yeah? Take care.

templates.expositionText: {target_npc} borrowed my {quest_item} a while back. I don't think they realize how long it's been. Could you swing by {target_npc_settlement} and bring it home?
templates.acceptText: That's a weight off. I've been too polite to bring it up myself.
templates.declineText: Fair point. I should just ask them myself.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Finally. I was starting to forget what it looked like.
templates.resolutionText: Thank you, I didn't want to make it awkward. Take {reward_item} with my thanks.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Has it really been that long? I completely lost track. Here's the {quest_item}, good as new. Mostly.
templates.targetNpcCloser: Give them my apologies. Safe travels.

templates.expositionText: I hate to be that person, but {target_npc} still has my {quest_item}. I lent it out meaning to get it back in a week. That was months ago. They're in {target_npc_settlement}.
templates.acceptText: Oh, thank you. I've been dreading the conversation and this spares me the awkwardness.
templates.declineText: I suppose a letter would do. If I can find a way to phrase it that doesn't sound passive-aggressive.
templates.expositionTurnInText: At last. I was running out of ways to politely not mention it.
templates.resolutionText: You're a diplomat. Here's {reward_item}. I'd resigned myself to never seeing this again, truthfully.
templates.targetNpcOpener: Oh no, the {quest_item}. I kept telling myself I'd bring it back. I'm terrible. Here, please take it.
templates.targetNpcCloser: I feel awful. Tell them I'm sorry. Really.

templates.expositionText: There's a bounty out on {boss_name}. They've been making the road south of {settlement_name} a bad place to walk. Anyone who puts them down gets the purse we put together.
templates.acceptText: Good. The notice has been up two weeks and nobody's even taken it down to read it twice.
templates.declineText: Can't blame you. It's a serious ask and the bounty's not what it ought to be.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You're sure? You got {boss_name}? I'll pull the notice down myself. That's a weight off this whole place.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item} with our thanks. Plenty of folk talk about collecting on notices like that. You did. That counts for something around here.

templates.expositionText: {boss_name} and their crew came through last month. Burned my barn down to the studs and laughed about it. I don't have it in me to chase them, but I've got coin set aside if you do.
templates.acceptText: Good. I want them dead. I'm tired of pretending I'm above saying it.
templates.declineText: Then I keep waiting. Maybe someone with less to lose comes through next.
templates.expositionTurnInText: It's done? Truly? I think I might sleep tonight without my hand on the door.
templates.resolutionText: Here. {reward_item}. I had coin set aside for the rebuild, and I'd rather spend it on the reason I can sleep tonight. The barn can wait another month.

templates.expositionText: {boss_name} has been working the area for half a year and we've lost three carts to them already. {settlement_npc} took an arrow last time. We're past the point of warnings.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I was starting to think we'd have to send our own people, and I don't want that on me.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'd rather not ask, but the alternative is worse.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a clean end to a long problem. {settlement_npc} will hear about it before sundown.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}, drawn from the hall's strongbox with {settlement_name}'s gratitude behind it. {settlement_npc} is the one who insisted the sum be honest.

templates.expositionText: I've been hearing the name {boss_name} for so long I'd half forgotten they were a real person and not just a story we tell to scare each other. Then they hit the next valley over last week. So. They're real, and they're close.
templates.acceptText: All right. All right. Maybe this gets to be the year someone settles it.
templates.declineText: Yeah, I figured. They're a name for a reason. I'll keep my head down.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You're telling me {boss_name} is gone. Just like that. After all these years. I don't know what to do with my hands.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. I'd been sitting on this purse so long I started thinking of it as ornamental. Turns out it was a bounty after all. Go on.

templates.expositionText: Ever since {boss_name} started raiding nearby, I haven't slept right. Every shadow is them. Every noise outside is them. I can't keep living like this.
templates.acceptText: Oh, thank you. Even hearing you say yes, my shoulders just dropped. I didn't realize how high they'd been.
templates.declineText: No, of course. It's a lot to ask. I'll keep my door barred and hope.
templates.expositionTurnInText: They're really gone? You're sure? I need a moment. I haven't been allowed to feel this in months.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}, please. I want it out of this house and in the hands of the person who made the house safe again. That's how I know it's real.

templates.expositionText: You look like someone who could handle {boss_name}. There's a head price on them and most of the locals here are farmers. Interested?
templates.acceptText: Excellent. Drinks are on me when you come back. If you come back.
templates.declineText: Fair enough. Some heads aren't worth the trip. I won't push.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Well, I'll be. You actually did it. I owe you the drinks I promised, on top of everything else.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item} for the job, as promised. I owe you a round at the bar on top of it, and I'm a man who pays his tab. Pleasure doing business.

templates.expositionText: Notice went up yesterday. A fair purse for {boss_name}, no questions how. Folk have been drinking slower and looking at it sideways all afternoon.
templates.acceptText: Good. I was tired of pretending that notice wasn't spoiling the mood in here.
templates.declineText: Understood. It'll sit there a while longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You came back. I'll get word to the collectors and take the notice down.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. The jar on the shelf is lighter by half tonight, and folk will drink faster knowing why.

templates.expositionText: The hall issued a writ on {boss_name} a fortnight back. It's not my writ, but I can point you at who's paying. You'd save us a lot of letters.
templates.acceptText: Very well. I'll send word ahead that it's being handled.
templates.declineText: No offense taken. Writs don't pay themselves down in an afternoon.
templates.expositionTurnInText: The writ can be closed, then. I'll see the seal broken before the week is out.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}, drawn against the writ and witnessed. I'll mark the seal broken tonight. Signed and settled.

templates.expositionText: We put coin on a nail for whoever takes {boss_name}. It's not much, but it's honest, and it's in my hand when you come back.
templates.acceptText: Good. Nail's been waiting for someone willing to take what's on it.
templates.declineText: That's your right. Coin'll stay nailed.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You're back. Hand's yours. The nail comes down tonight.
templates.resolutionText: Here's {reward_item}, off the nail and into your hand like I said. Honest coin for an honest end. You earned every bit.

templates.expositionText: I keep a standing offer on {boss_name}. Had it up since before I took this shop over, if you'd believe it. Whoever clears the name gets paid, no questions.
templates.acceptText: At last. I'd just about given up on ever paying that purse out.
templates.declineText: Understandable. Standing offers don't have a deadline for a reason.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Years it took, and you did it. Doesn't feel real, honestly.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}, paid out of a purse older than most folk drinking here. Feels strange to hand it over. Good strange. Never thought I'd see the day.

templates.expositionText: My brother was on the cart {boss_name}'s crew hit in autumn. He didn't come home. Coin's not what I'm paying you for, but it's what I have.
templates.acceptText: Good. That's the closest I'll get to an answer.
templates.declineText: Then I'll keep waiting. Somebody, someday.
templates.expositionTurnInText: It's done. I don't feel what I thought I'd feel. But it's done.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. My brother would've said it was too much for any job. I'm finding I disagree with him tonight, and I'm allowed to.

templates.expositionText: {boss_name} and their lot salted my west field on purpose. On purpose. Just to watch me kneel and cry. I've got coin set aside and I want to spend it on this.
templates.acceptText: Finally. I've been carrying this in my teeth for a year.
templates.declineText: Then I keep carrying it. I've done worse.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Gone. Good. Let them plow dirt in whatever's next.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}, and don't argue about what it's worth. My wife was saving that silver for a better year. This is the better year. Take it and go eat somewhere well lit.

templates.expositionText: I don't talk about what {boss_name} did to my family and I won't start now. I have coin, and I have a name, and I'm asking if you'll finish this for us.
templates.acceptText: Yes. That's the right answer and the only one I could stand to hear.
templates.declineText: Then I go looking for the next person who walks through.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You did what I couldn't. There's no clean way to say thanks for that.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}. Mine to give, and given freely. Don't say anything kind back. I won't hold.

templates.expositionText: {boss_name} stood over my son with the same smile the whole time. He lived. They laughed. I want that smile gone from the world, and I'll pay whatever I have to.
templates.acceptText: Good. I don't care how it gets done, only that it does.
templates.declineText: Then I'll look at someone else until they say yes.
templates.expositionTurnInText: It's off the world, then. That's all I wanted.
templates.resolutionText: Here. {reward_item}, everything I promised and then some if I could find a way. Worth every piece to hear that smile is gone.

templates.expositionText: The council voted this morning. {boss_name} has to go, or we board up the eastern road for winter. Neither option is free. Yours is the one we're hoping to pay for.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'll tell the council we have someone.
templates.declineText: Then we'll take the vote again. We know the answer, I just hate hearing it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Word's already traveled. Two families took their boards down off the windows.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item} from the common purse. Every household put something in. The council argued about the sum. Nobody's arguing now.

templates.expositionText: We lose {boss_name} or we lose the road. We've been rationing flour for two weeks because carters won't come past the bend anymore. I need someone to fix this before we start running thin.
templates.acceptText: Good. I'll get word to the mill that relief is coming.
templates.declineText: Fair. But the flour doesn't care what's fair.
templates.expositionTurnInText: The first cart rolled in this afternoon. The baker nearly cried.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. The baker's got flour on her apron again. The road feeds us because you walked it. That's not small.

templates.expositionText: I've been warden of {settlement_name} fourteen years and I cannot put {boss_name} down by myself. I hate the words as I say them. The coin we pooled says them for me.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'd rather admit it once than keep pretending.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll keep pretending a while longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: I'll put it in the ledger tonight. With your name, not mine.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item} from the pool we raised, and every coin of it owed to you before it was owed to anyone else. Fourteen years of warden's pay couldn't have bought what you did today.

templates.topicHeader: Before the Next One Gets Hurt
templates.expositionText: We've been lucky so far. {boss_name}'s been hitting carts, not homes. That luck will run out. I'm asking before it does, not after.
templates.acceptText: Good. Luck is a terrible thing to plan around.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll try to get the patrols doubled in the meantime.
templates.expositionTurnInText: One fewer thing to lose sleep over. I'll post the relief at the gate.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}, every piece of it from a family who had something left to lose. You spent their fear for them. That's the only reason I can hand this over with a clear head.

templates.expositionText: My father used to tell me to stay off the north road because of {boss_name}. I thought he was being dramatic. Now I'm the age he was and I tell my children the same thing.
templates.acceptText: So it gets to end with you. All right. Good.
templates.declineText: Then it gets told to one more generation. That's all right too.
templates.expositionTurnInText: My children won't have to learn the name. That's a gift I didn't think I'd give.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. It's out of a jar my father buried against this exact day and never lived to open. He'd have approved of where it ended up.

templates.expositionText: Somewhere around the fourth missing neighbor I stopped counting and started drinking more. That's {boss_name}'s doing. If you can put them down, I can start counting something better.
templates.acceptText: Right. Good. That's an answer I can work with.
templates.declineText: Fair. I wasn't expecting it to be you anyway.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Huh. I thought I'd feel more. Maybe the feeling shows up in a week.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}. I'd been saving a drink for a night like this and I'm not sure anymore that I want it. Maybe I'll count empty bottles instead of empty chairs.

templates.expositionText: Nobody alive in {settlement_name} can tell you what {boss_name} looks like. Just the name, and what gets done under it. I've always assumed it was more than one person wearing the same mask.
templates.acceptText: Works for me. Whatever face you find under the name.
templates.declineText: All right. Maybe the next traveler has a better look in their eye.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You saw a face. That alone is news.
templates.resolutionText: Here's {reward_item}. Whoever the face belonged to, you're the first in {settlement_name} to see it. That's worth more than the coin, honestly.

templates.expositionText: Children here grow up on stories of {boss_name}. The ones told so you'll believe in locking the door. I'm tired of being a story. Make us something else.
templates.acceptText: Good. The children can have a different story tonight.
templates.declineText: Fair. Stories keep, even the bad ones.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You're in the story now. That might be worse, for you.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. The lockboxes in {settlement_name} are sitting open for the first time in a year. You're welcome in the telling, whatever version of you wins out.

templates.expositionText: I've lost weight since {boss_name} started turning up nearby. Eating feels like something you do when you're safe. Could you make it safe?
templates.acceptText: You mean it? Oh. Oh, good.
templates.declineText: No, I hear you, I do. I'll keep eating what I can.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Gone. Truly gone. I'm going to sit down and eat until I can't.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}. There's stew on the hearth if you want a bowl before you go. No more hoarding. I'm eating again.

templates.expositionText: My kids haven't played outside in a month because of {boss_name}. They're quieter than children should be. If you can make it so they can run around again, you'll have a friend in this house forever.
templates.acceptText: I could kiss you. I'll tell them they can go out tomorrow.
templates.declineText: I understand. I'll keep them in a little longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: They're outside right now. Shrieking, I might add. It's the best sound I've ever heard.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. You've got a friend in this house forever, like I said. Come back when you can hear the noise they make. That's the thanks, really.

templates.topicHeader: I Hear Them in the Walls
templates.expositionText: Every creak is them. Every wind is them. I know it's in my head, mostly. But {boss_name} put it there, and I don't know how to get it out on my own.
templates.acceptText: Oh. Thank you. Thank you. Please.
templates.declineText: All right. I'll try breathing slower.
templates.expositionTurnInText: The house sounds like a house again. I didn't know houses were supposed to sound like anything.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}, please. I'd been clutching things like they could keep me safe. Turns out the only thing that did it was somebody else going out into the dark. That's you.

templates.topicHeader: My Wife Won't Answer the Door
templates.expositionText: My wife won't answer the door anymore. Not since {boss_name} started being in the area. I don't know if it's fear or pride or both. Either way, she deserves to open her own door again.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'll tell her there's hope, though I'll say it gently.
templates.declineText: That's fine. I'll get the door myself for a while longer.
templates.expositionTurnInText: She opened it this morning. Just the morning air, but she opened it.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. She wanted to hand it over herself, but she's still getting used to the threshold being safe to cross. Give her another week.

templates.topicHeader: Easy Money if You're Any Good
templates.expositionText: Coin's on {boss_name}'s head and most of the folk who come through here aren't going to collect it. You might. If I'm wrong, I'm out nothing.
templates.acceptText: Excellent. Come back thirsty, we'll settle up over drinks.
templates.declineText: Fair. I'll ask the next armed boot that walks in.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Would you look at that. The betting book was wrong for once.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}, then. I took the long odds on you and the bookie across the road is going to be sour about it all week. I'll drink on that for a month.

templates.expositionText: Listen, keep this between us. There's private coin on {boss_name} nobody's supposed to know about. Doesn't ask questions, doesn't leave a paper trail. Interested?
templates.acceptText: Smart. You'll make more this way than through the hall.
templates.declineText: Walk it off then. Never heard of me.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Told you. The folk who know about this don't shout it.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item}, quiet as it came. Don't ask where the stamp's from, don't mention this conversation. That's the whole deal.

templates.expositionText: Local bounty hunters have all had a crack at {boss_name}. None of them came back with the right result. You look new enough that it might surprise them.
templates.acceptText: Good. Surprise is half the work, the other half is not dying.
templates.declineText: Suit yourself. The local lot will keep trying and keep failing.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Well, I was right about you and wrong about them. Both feel good.
templates.resolutionText: {reward_item} for the job, and I'll be spending half of tonight watching the hunters' guild pretend this didn't happen. That alone was worth the wager.

templates.expositionText: Purse on {boss_name} rolls over every month someone doesn't claim it. It's at an embarrassing number now. I want to see it claimed before I have to sell a cart to fund the next round.
templates.acceptText: Thank you, truly. The ledger's been a headache for too long.
templates.declineText: Worth a shot. I'll add another cart to the list I'm pretending to own.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Finally. I'll close this page of the ledger and never open it again.
templates.resolutionText: Take {reward_item}. The purse rolls over at month's end and I'd rather see it paid out than swell any larger. I'm closing this page of the ledger tonight.
